creator: vadim@gc.lviv.ua
text:
In netatalk+asun in original paths.h looks:
#ifndef _PATH_LOCKDIR
#ifdef BSD4_4
#define _PATH_LOCKDIR           "/var/spool/lock/"
#else
#ifdef linux
#define _PATH_LOCKDIR           "/var/lock/"
#endif /* linux */
#endif /* BSD4_4 */
#endif

In patch-ah added:
 #else
-#define _PATH_LOCKDIR           "/var/spool/locks/"
+#define _PATH_LOCKDIR           "/var/run/"

But it never take in effect because of FreeBSD is in BSD4_4 family
(see <sys/param.h>) and path are /var/spool/locks

Because of  /usr/local/etc/rc.d/netatalk.sh stop doesn't work.
And more: combination start, stop, start freese mashine: it doesn't 
responce to ping and keyboard. But it seems to kernell bug?

Fix: 

I propose paths.h:
#include <sys/param.h>
...
...
/* lock file path. this should be re-organized a bit. */
#ifndef _PATH_LOCKDIR
#ifdef __FreeBSD__
#define _PATH_LOCKDIR           "/var/run/"
#else
#ifdef BSD4_4
#define _PATH_LOCKDIR           "/var/spool/lock/"
#else
#ifdef linux
#define _PATH_LOCKDIR           "/var/lock/"
#endif /* FreeBSD */
#endif /* linux */
#endif /* BSD4_4 */
#endif
How-To-Repeat: Install from ports netatalk-asun. Run daemons: 
/usr/local/etc/rc.d/netatalk.sh start
afpd.pid, papd.pid, atalkd.pid are in /usr/local/lock instead of
/var/run.
